notes: Wald mean-coefficient intervals calibrated — docs/dev-log/simulation-artifacts/2026-07-09-nongaussian-unstructured-coverage-pilot

Blanket gate on spec$model_type != "gaussian" fires FIRST, before any per-term formula validation. Represents ALL beta x REML rows in this census (every dpar, effect_type, and structure_provider) -- not re-enumerated per row below.
Wald scale-coefficient intervals calibrated for INTERIOR proportions — docs/dev-log/simulation-artifacts/2026-07-09-nongaussian-unstructured-coverage-pilot (phase 2); exact 0/1 need zero_one_beta()
Same blanket gate as the mu-fixed REML row.

Unlabelled (1 | id) term; single-seed sim shows cor(id_effects, truth) > 0.45 and profile_targets() flags sd:mu:(1 | id) profile_ready=TRUE, but no confint()/profile() is actually run in tests, so kept below interval_feasible.
Covered by the blanket mu-fixed REML row above.
Unlabelled independent numeric slope (0 + x | id); cor(slope_effects, truth) > 0.25 in a single-seed sim across families incl. beta; profile target present but no confint/profile executed.
Covered by the blanket mu-fixed REML row above.

CORRELATED/labelled variant of the mu random slope -- (1 + x | id) errors "Only independent...", (1 | p | id) errors "random intercepts". Distinct from the row above (independent slope, which IS implemented); same effect_type value because the axis schema has no separate "correlated" category.

Non-Gaussian sigma random effects are not implemented for beta -- unconditional cli_abort for ANY sigma random-effect term (intercept or slope, labelled or not). REML would fail independently before reaching this check (see sigma-fixed REML row); no separate REML row added since ML already rejects this shape.

Same unconditional validator as the sigma ordinary_re_intercept row; listed separately only to keep the mu/sigma x intercept/slope grid parallel. No dedicated slope test exists for beta sigma, but the validator rejects on term presence alone, independent of shape.

notes: Intercept-only (q1) unlabelled animal() term in mu. Board fit_status=point_fit, interval_status=unsupported, coverage_status=planned. Board cell_id retains a historical "_rejected" suffix even though the current smoke (tools/qseries-v1-first-four-rejection-smoke.R) reports status=expected_fit for it. No interval/coverage evidence -> point_fit_recovery, not higher.

One-slope variant: intercept + one numeric slope, INDEPENDENT/uncorrelated variance components (no covariance_label permitted). Board labels this dimension_pattern=q1 / slope_class=independent_one_slope (not a true labelled q2 covariance block). Recovery-only crossed n_lvl{10,20,30} x 30-seed ladder bundling beta with gamma/student: 90/90 beta fits converged with pdHess=TRUE. No interval, coverage, labelled, or multi-slope claim.

Representative row for ALL beyond-admitted animal mu shapes: labelled/correlated blocks at any true q, and unlabelled terms with 2+ slopes (e.g. animal(1 + x1 + x2 | id)). Every such shape hits the same cli_abort ("only unlabelled q=1 intercepts" / "only ... intercept-only or one-slope") regardless of its real covariance size, so q_gate=q2 here is a placeholder, not a validated q2 block.

Intercept-only (q1) unlabelled animal() term in sigma only. Board fit_status=point_fit, interval_status=unsupported, coverage_status=planned. mu and sigma animal effects cannot be requested simultaneously (R/drmTMB.R:4408-4415 aborts "beta structured animal effects currently support one endpoint at a time" -- q2/q4 beta location-scale blocks are explicitly post-v1.0 design work).

Representative row for one-slope, labelled, or larger unlabelled animal sigma terms. Sigma is stricter than mu: ONLY intercept-only is admitted, no one-slope allowance at all (unlike mu). Confirmed directly by test: sigma ~ animal(1 + x | id, pedigree = ped) -> "intercept-only structured terms".
